practicum.description: Two semesters of supervised practicum experience at Rutgers ABA training sites (DDDC or RCAAS), meeting BACB supervised fieldwork requirements.

capstone_thesis_option.description: Students choose between a capstone or thesis route. Capstone consists of two courses (6 credits); thesis is a single 3-credit course plus independent research.

electives.description: Select elective ABA courses to complete 42 total credits. With capstone (6cr): 1 elective needed. With thesis (3cr): 2 electives needed.

bacb_eligibility.description: Coursework aligns with the BACB Fifth Edition Task List and meets BACB academic requirements for BCBA certification. Students must also complete supervised fieldwork hours and pass the BCBA examination.
notes: Completion of MABA alone does not guarantee BCBA certification eligibility.
Students should track fieldwork hours via the BACB Experience Tracker.
Practicum at DDDC and RCAAS counts toward supervised independent fieldwork hours.

training_sites: Douglass Developmental Disabilities Center (DDDC) — serving individuals with autism spectrum disorder across the lifespan
Rutgers Center for Adult Autism Services (RCAAS) — focused on adult ASD support and employment
constraints: Total: 42 credits (27 required core + 6 practicum + 6 capstone or 3 thesis + electives).
PPSY510 (Practicum I) requires concurrent enrollment with or prior completion of PPSY513.
PPSY521 (Ethics) is required and must be taken before advanced ABA courses.
Classes held evenings (3:35 p.m. or 6:15 p.m.) to accommodate working professionals.
notes: Unique integration of didactic coursework with hands-on clinical training in nationally recognized ABA centers.
Thesis track is recommended for students considering doctoral study.

map_mtss.major_name: Applied Psychology in Multi-Tiered Systems of Support (MAP-MTSS)

gse_required_courses.description: Two required courses from the Rutgers Graduate School of Education
notes: SPED523 — Teaching in Inclusive Classrooms (GSE 15:293:523, Spring, 3 credits)
GSE classroom organization course (15:293:TBD, Fall, 3 credits) — confirm with advisor for current offering

additional_required_courses.description: Additional MAP-MTSS required courses beyond certificate requirements
notes: MTSS in Secondary Educational Settings (18:820:540 — confirm current availability)
Multi-Tiered Social Emotional Learning and Behavioral Supports (18:820:TBD)

constraints: Total: 30 credits (18 certificate credits + 12 additional master's credits).
Fully online asynchronous format; students typically take 2 courses per semester.
PPSY533 requires a weekly virtual synchronous session with the instructor.
Prerequisites: bachelor's degree with coursework in education or psychology; teaching or school experience strongly preferred.
notes: The MAP-MTSS builds on the 18-credit Certificate in MTSS, adding deeper coursework in data-based decision making and school mental health.
